venues.name: Daytona International Speedway
venues.description: The birthplace of NASCAR. Two-and-a-half miles of high-banked superspeedway drama. Home of the Daytona 500 — the Great American Race. Restrictor plate racing at 200mph with inches between 40 cars.

venues.name: Atlanta Motor Speedway
venues.description: Repaved and reconfigured in 2022 to create a high-speed superspeedway-style experience. Now the second fastest track on the circuit. Pack racing at 190mph makes it one of the most chaotic weekends of the year.

venues.name: Las Vegas Motor Speedway
venues.description: The quintessential mile-and-a-half oval. Smooth, fast and technical. Strong pit strategy circuit where track position is everything. Two Cup races per year.

venues.name: Phoenix Raceway
venues.description: The championship decider. Phoenix hosts the penultimate race of the NASCAR playoffs and crowns the Cup Series champion. A flat, D-shaped one-mile oval where setup precision wins races.

venues.name: Bristol Motor Speedway (Dirt)
venues.description: Once a year Bristol transforms into a dirt track. The Food City Dirt Race brings mud, mayhem and carnage. 40 cars on a tiny bullring with no grip — pure survival racing.

venues.name: Circuit of the Americas
venues.description: The longest circuit on the NASCAR calendar. F1-spec road course in the Texas Hill Country. Demands a completely different setup to ovals. Chaos at Turn 1 on lap one is basically guaranteed.

venues.name: Martinsville Speedway
venues.description: The oldest active track in NASCAR. A paperclip-shaped half-mile where bump-drafting, door banging and revenge are the order of the day. Winners receive a grandfather clock. Locals call it the world's longest hot dog.

venues.name: Talladega Superspeedway
venues.description: The biggest, baddest, fastest oval in NASCAR. 2.66 miles of high-banked Alabama superspeedway where the Big One lurks every lap. Anyone can win at Talladega — and anyone can get wrecked.

venues.name: Dover Motor Speedway
venues.description: The Monster Mile. Concrete one-mile oval with 24-degree banking that generates massive tire wear. Passing is difficult but the high speeds make for intense racing. The Monster energy drinks car mascot stands watch at the start-finish line.

venues.name: Kansas Speedway
venues.description: One of the newer 1.5-mile ovals on the circuit. Wide, smooth racing surface allows multiple lanes. Playoff staple that often produces dramatic finishes.

venues.name: Charlotte Motor Speedway
venues.description: Home track for most of NASCAR's teams. The Coca-Cola 600 on Memorial Day weekend is the longest race on the calendar at 600 miles. Charlotte also hosts the All-Star Race and a road course variant.

venues.name: Pocono Raceway
venues.description: The Tricky Triangle. A unique triangular 2.5-mile oval with three completely different corners — Tunnel Turn, the Carousel and the North Turn. No other track in NASCAR feels like Pocono.

venues.name: Michigan International Speedway
venues.description: Two miles of smooth, fast Michigan asphalt. One of the widest tracks on the circuit allows for multi-lane racing. Top speeds approach 220mph making it one of the most technically demanding ovals.

venues.name: Sonoma Raceway
venues.description: Wine country road racing. Sonoma's rolling hills and technical layout favour road course specialists. The carousel sequence is one of the most photographed corners in American motorsport.

venues.name: New Hampshire Motor Speedway
venues.description: The Magic Mile. Flat, low-banked New Hampshire demands precision and patience. A track where setup and tire management matter more than raw speed.

venues.name: Watkins Glen International
venues.description: Historic road course in the Finger Lakes. One of the oldest circuits in American racing. The bus stop chicane and the boot section create wheel-to-wheel moments that flat ovals can't match.

venues.name: Indianapolis Motor Speedway
venues.description: The Racing Capital of the World. NASCAR races both the oval and the road course at Indy. Standing at the yard of bricks at the start-finish line is a pilgrimage every racing fan should make.

venues.name: Richmond Raceway
venues.description: Three-quarters of a mile of short-track action under the lights. Richmond's Saturday night race tradition stretches back decades. Two races per year make it a double-dose of tight, tactical racing.

venues.name: Daytona International Speedway (Road Course)
venues.description: The infield road course at Daytona uses part of the famous oval banking in combination with an infield section. A unique challenge that mixes superspeedway banking with technical infield corners.

venues.name: Bristol Motor Speedway
venues.description: The Last Great Colosseum. Half-mile concrete bullring with 28-degree banking makes Bristol the most electric short-track atmosphere in racing. The night race under the lights is a bucket list event.
